trigger_cron.label: CRON expression
trigger_cron.tooltip: Exactly 5 space separated segments, in standard crontab rules.
trigger_cron.help: Expected execution time for the last 5 times (the actual time zone is based on the server):
trigger_cron.guide:
If you have multiple workflows, it is recommended to set them to run at different times of the day instead of always running at a specific time. And please don't always set it to midnight every day to avoid spikes in traffic.

Reference links:
1. Let’s Encrypt rate limits
2. Why should my Let’s Encrypt (ACME) client run at a random time?

domain.label: Domains
domain.description:
• Supports single-domain, multi-domain and wildcard domains (Depends on CA).
  • Supports DNS-01 or HTTP-01 challenge.

ip.label: IP addresses
ip.description:
• Supports IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.
  • Only supports HTTP-01 challenge.
  • Default configuration: No common name.
  • Default configuration: CA is Let's Encrypt.
  • Default configuration: ACME profile is shortlived.
  • Shorter certificate validity and renewal interval.

domains.label: Domains
domains.help: Notes: Multiple domains should be separated by semicolons. Wildcard domain should be written as *.example.com.
domains.help_no_wildcard: Notes: Multiple domains should be separated by semicolons.

ipaddrs.label: IP addresses
ipaddrs.placeholder: Please enter IP addresses (separated by semicolons)
ipaddrs.help: Notes: Multiple IP addresses should be separated by semicolons.

contact_email.label: Contact email
contact_email.tooltip: Contact information required for SSL certificate application. Please pay attention to the rate limits.

challenge_type.label: Challenge type

errmsg.no_wildcard_in_http01: Could not use HTTP-01 challenge to request wildcard domain certificates.
errmsg.no_ip_in_dns01: Could not use DNS-01 challenge to request IP address certificates.

challenge_type.tooltip:
It determines how the CAs verifies your control over the domain names.
Click here to learn more.

provider_dns01.label: DNS provider
provider_dns01.placeholder: Please select the DNS provider of the domains

provider_http01.label: Hosting provider
provider_http01.placeholder: Please select the hosting provider of the domains

provider_access.label: Provider credential
provider_access.placeholder: Please select an credential of the provider

provider_access_dns01.label: DNS provider credential
provider_access_dns01.placeholder: Please select an credential of the DNS provider

provider_access_http01.label: Hosting provider credential
provider_access_http01.placeholder: Please select an credential of the hosting provider

reuse.label: Reuse
reuse.help: Notes: If there is an existing certificate, the original key algorithm will be used.

custom.label: Custom
custom.help: Notes: Please ensure that the algorithm matches the private key.

errmsg.not_matched: Private key does not match the key algorithm (Expected: {{expected}}, Actual: {{actual}})

ca_provider_access.label: Certificate authority credential
ca_provider_access.placeholder: Please select an credential of the certificate authority

validity_lifetime.label: Validity lifetime (Optional)
validity_lifetime.help: Notes: Not all CAs support this feature.
validity_lifetime.tooltip: It determines the NotAfter field of the certificate in the ACME protocol. If you don't understand this option, just keep it by default.

preferred_chain.label: Preferred chain (Optional)
preferred_chain.help: Notes: Not all CAs support this feature.
preferred_chain.tooltip:
It determines the PreferredChain field of the certificate in the ACME protocol. If you don't understand this option, just keep it by default.
Click here to learn more.

acme_profile.label: ACME profile (Optional)
acme_profile.help: Notes: Not all CAs support this feature.
acme_profile.tooltip:
It determines the Profile field of the certificate in the ACME protocol. If you don't understand this option, just keep it by default.
Click here to learn more.

disable_cn.label: Disable common name in CSR
disable_cn.tooltip:
It determines whether to include the Subject.CommonName field in the certificate. If you don't understand this option, just keep it by default.
Click here to learn more.

nameservers.label: DNS recursive nameservers (Optional)
nameservers.placeholder: Please enter DNS recursive nameservers (separated by semicolons)
nameservers.tooltip:
It determines whether to custom DNS recursive nameservers during ACME challenge. If you don't understand this option, just keep it by default.
Click here to learn more.

dns_propagation_wait.label: DNS propagation waiting time (Optional)
dns_propagation_wait.placeholder: Please enter DNS propagation waiting time
dns_propagation_wait.tooltip: It determines the waiting time for DNS propagation during ACME DNS-01 challenge. If you don't understand this option, just keep it by default.

dns_propagation_timeout.label: DNS propagation checks timeout (Optional)
dns_propagation_timeout.placeholder: Please enter DNS propagation checks timeout
dns_propagation_timeout.tooltip: It determines the timeout for DNS propagation checks during ACME DNS-01 challenge. If you don't understand this option, just keep it by default.

dns_ttl.label: DNS TTL (Optional)
dns_ttl.help: Notes: Leave it blank to use the default value provided by the DNS provider.
dns_ttl.tooltip: It determines the TTL for DNS record during ACME DNS-01 challenge. If you don't understand this option, just keep it by default.

http_delay_wait.label: HTTP delay waiting time (Optional)
http_delay_wait.placeholder: Please enter HTTP delay waiting time
http_delay_wait.tooltip: It determines a delay between the start of the HTTP server and the challenge validation during ACME HTTP-01 challenge. If you don't understand this option, just keep it by default.

disable_follow_cname.label: Disable CNAME following
disable_follow_cname.tooltip:
It determines whether to disable CNAME following during ACME DNS-01 challenge. If you don't understand this option, just keep it by default.
Click here to learn more.

disable_ari.label: Disable ARI
disable_ari.tooltip:
It determines whether to disable ARI (ACME Renewal Information). If you don't understand this option, just keep it by default.
Click here to learn more.

skip_before_expiry_days.label: Repeated application
skip_before_expiry_days.prefix: Perform renewal if the last issued certificate expires in ≤
skip_before_expiry_days.help: Notes: The actual renewal interval also depends on the CRON expression that triggers the workflow.

ssh_webroot_path.label: Web root path
ssh_webroot_path.tooltip: It's the main directory where the website's files are stored on the server.

ssh_use_scp.label: Fallback to use SCP
ssh_use_scp.tooltip: If the remote server does not support SFTP, please check this option to fallback to SCP.

form.guide: The file content will be read again every time this node executes.

certificate_path.placeholder: Please enter the local path for certificate file

certificate_url.label: Certificate file URL
certificate_url.placeholder: Please enter the URL for downloading certificate file

private_key_url.placeholder: Please enter the URL for downloading private key file

form.guide: It will send a HEAD request to the target address to obtain the certificate. Please ensure that the address is accessible through HTTPS protocol.

domain.label: Domain (Optional)
domain.help: Notes: Only required when the host is an IP address.

certificate_output_node_id.label: Certificate to deploy
certificate_output_node_id.help: Notes: The certificate to be deployed comes from the previous nodes of application or upload.

provider_access.label: Hosting provider credential
provider_access.placeholder: Please select an credential of the hosting provider

skip_on_last_succeeded.label: Repeated deployment
skip_on_last_succeeded.prefix: If the last deployment was successful,

exact.label: Exact matches
exact.help.wildcard: Notes: For the sites which support wildcard resolution, an exact match of a wildcard domain only includes the site itself, does not include its subdomains.

shared_script_command.vartips:
Supported variables:
  1. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_CMDVAR_CERTIFICATE_PATH}:
    The path of the certificate file, same as the value of the form related field.
  2. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_CMDVAR_CERTIFICATE_SERVER_PATH}:
    The path of the server certificate file, same as the value of the form related field.
  3. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_CMDVAR_CERTIFICATE_INTERMEDIA_PATH}:
    The path of the intermediate CA certificate file, same as the value of the form related field.
  4. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_CMDVAR_PRIVATEKEY_PATH}:
    The path of the private key file, same as the value of the form related field.
  5. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_CMDVAR_PFX_PASSWORD}:
    The PFX password, same as the value of the form related field.
  6. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_CMDVAR_JKS_ALIAS}:
    The JKS alias, same as the value of the form related field.
  7. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_CMDVAR_JKS_KEYPASS}:
    The JKS key password, same as the value of the form related field.
  8. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_CMDVAR_JKS_STOREPASS}:
    The JKS store password, same as the value of the form related field.

shared_file_path_for_key.label: Private key file path
shared_file_path_for_key.placeholder: Please enter output file path for private key file
shared_file_path_for_key.help: Notes: It should include the full file path, not just the directory.

shared_file_path_for_crt.placeholder: Please enter output file path for certificate
shared_file_path_for_crt.help: Notes: It should include the full file path, not just the directory.

shared_file_path_for_servercrt.placeholder: Please enter output file path for server certificate file
shared_file_path_for_servercrt.help: Notes: It should include the full file path, not just the directory.

shared_file_path_for_intermediacrt.label: Certificate file (includes the CA parts only) path (Optional)
shared_file_path_for_intermediacrt.placeholder: Please enter output file path for intermediate certificate file
shared_file_path_for_intermediacrt.help: Notes: It should include the full file path, not just the directory.

webhook_data.vartips:
Supported variables:
  1. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_COMMONNAME}:
    The primary domain or IP address of the certificate.
  2. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_SUBJECTALTNAMES}:
    The domains or IP addresses of the certificate, separated by semicolons.
  3. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_CERTIFICATE}:
    The PEM format content of the certificate file.
  4. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_CERTIFICATE_SERVER}:
    The PEM format content of the server certificate file.
  5. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_CERTIFICATE_INTERMEDIA}:
    The PEM format content of the intermediate CA certificate file.
  6. ${CERTIMATE_DEPLOYER_PRIVATEKEY}:
    The PEM format content of the private key file.

template.guide:
The content using the "Mustache" syntax (double curly braces) and preceded by "$" in the subject or message are text interpolations. They will be replaced by the actual values.
Supported text interpolations:
  1. Workflow:
    1. workflow.id: The ID of the workflow.
    2. workflow.name: The name of the workflow.
    3. workflow.description: The description of the workflow.
    4. run.id: The ID of the workflow run.
  2. Error:
    (If there are multiple nodes that have failed before this, it always indicate the nearest one.)
    1. error.nodeId: The node ID that execution failed.
    2. error.nodeName: The node name that execution failed.
    3. error.message: The error message that execution failed.
  3. Certificate:
    (If there are multiple nodes outputting a certificate before this, it always indicate the nearest one.)
    1. certificate.commonName: The primary domain or IP address of the certificate.
    2. certificate.subjectAltNames: The domains or IP addresses of the certificate, separated by semicolons.
    3. certificate.notBefore: The effect time of the certificate, formatted in RFC3339.
    4. certificate.notAfter: The expire time of the certificate, formatted in RFC3339.
    5. certificate.hoursLeft: The left hours of the certificate.
    6. certificate.daysLeft: The left days of the certificate.
    7. certificate.validity: The validity of the certificate.
  4. Other:
    1. now: The current time on the server, formatted in RFC3339.

Example:
Your workflow {{ $workflow.name }} has failed on node {{ $error.nodeName }} at {{ $now }}.
